title: urera oligoloba var. papangensis
intro: Urera oligoloba var. papangensis, a rare and lesser-known variety of the Urera oligoloba plant, is a species of flowering plant in the nettle family (Urticaceae). It is traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the Philippines, where its medicinal properties are valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic effects. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including lowering blood sugar levels, reducing blood pressure, and alleviating symptoms of fever and rheumatism. Urera oligoloba var. papangensis is typically prepared as an infusion or decoction, and is also used in traditional herbal remedies such as teas, tonics, and salves.
